InvestorProfile.title: InvestorProfile
InvestorProfile.description: Represents an individual investor's profile within the application, providing basic contact and account details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvestorProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the investor.

lastName.description: The last name of the investor.

email.description: The email address of the investor, used for identification and communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the investor profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the investor profile was last updated.

Investment.title: Investment
Investment.description: Represents a single financial holding within an investor's portfolio, detailing its acquisition, type, and current market value.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Investment entity.

investorId.description: Reference to the InvestorProfile who owns this investment. (Relationship: InvestorProfile 1:N Investment)

name.description: A descriptive name for the investment (e.g., 'Apple Stock', 'Vanguard S&P 500 ETF').

symbol.description: The ticker symbol or identifier for the investment (e.g., 'AAPL' for Apple stock). Optional for some investment types.

type.description: The category or type of the investment (e.g., 'Stock', 'Bond', 'Mutual Fund', 'ETF', 'Cryptocurrency').

purchaseDate.description: The date and time when the investment was purchased.

purchasePricePerUnit.description: The price per unit (e.g., per share) at the time of purchase.

quantity.description: The total number of units (e.g., shares, coins) purchased.

currency.description: The currency in which the investment was made and is valued (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'JPY').

currentMarketPricePerUnit.description: The latest known market price per unit of the investment.

lastPriceUpdate.description: Timestamp of the last update for 'currentMarketPricePerUnit'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the investment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the investment record was last updated.

InvestmentDailyMetric.title: InvestmentDailyMetric
InvestmentDailyMetric.description: Stores daily calculated performance metrics for an individual investment, used for historical charting and analysis of single asset performance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvestmentDailyMetric entity.

investmentId.description: Reference to the Investment this metric belongs to. (Relationship: Investment 1:N InvestmentDailyMetric)

metricDate.description: The specific date for which these daily metrics are recorded.

closingPricePerUnit.description: The closing market price per unit of the investment on the 'metricDate'.

dailyGainLossAmount.description: The monetary gain or loss for this specific investment on the 'metricDate'.

dailyGainLossPercentage.description: The percentage gain or loss for this specific investment on the 'metricDate'.

totalValueAtClose.description: The total market value of the investment (calculated as 'closingPricePerUnit' * 'quantity' from Investment) at the close of 'metricDate'.

PortfolioDailyMetric.title: PortfolioDailyMetric
PortfolioDailyMetric.description: Stores daily calculated performance metrics for an investor's entire portfolio, providing historical data for overall portfolio trend visualization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioDailyMetric entity.

investorId.description: Reference to the InvestorProfile whose portfolio these metrics represent. (Relationship: InvestorProfile 1:N PortfolioDailyMetric)

metricDate.description: The specific date for which these daily portfolio metrics are recorded.

totalPortfolioValue.description: The aggregate market value of all investments in the investor's portfolio at the close of 'metricDate'.

totalInvestedAmount.description: The cumulative sum of initial purchase prices for all active investments in the portfolio up to 'metricDate'.

dailyGainLossAmount.description: The total monetary gain or loss for the entire portfolio on the 'metricDate'.

dailyGainLossPercentage.description: The total percentage gain or loss for the entire portfolio on the 'metricDate'.

overallGainLossAmount.description: The total monetary gain or loss for the portfolio from its inception up to 'metricDate'.

overallGainLossPercentage.description: The total percentage gain or loss for the portfolio from its inception up to 'metricDate'.

definition.description: Stores a single investor's profile, uniquely identified by their Firebase Authentication UID. The `{investorId}` path parameter MUST match `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: investorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the investor, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual investments owned by a specific investor. The document's `investorId` field MUST match the `{investorId}` path parameter and `request.auth.uid`. This ensures authorization independence.

params.name: investorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the investor who owns the investment.

params.name: investmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific investment.

definition.description: Stores daily performance metrics for a specific investment. The document MUST include a denormalized 'investorId' field (from the parent InvestorProfile) and its 'investmentId' field MUST match the '{investmentId}' path parameter. Both 'investorId' from the document and the path '{investorId}' MUST match 'request.auth.uid' for authorization independence.

params.name: investorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the investor who owns the parent investment.

params.name: investmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent investment.

params.name: metricId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific daily investment metric.

definition.description: Stores daily performance metrics for an investor's entire portfolio. The document's `investorId` field MUST match the `{investorId}` path parameter and `request.auth.uid`. This ensures authorization independence.

params.name: investorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the investor whose portfolio these metrics represent.

params.name: metricId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific daily portfolio metric.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure for PROFITABLEFIRM prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access based on the core design principles. All data is user-private, secured by path-based ownership. The root collection `/investorProfiles` maps directly to Firebase Authentication UIDs, ensuring that each investor's data is isolated and directly accessible only by them. Authorization Independence (CRITICAL): This design eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules. This is achieved through strategic denormalization: 1.  **InvestorProfile:** The document ID in `/investorProfiles/{investorId}` is the `request.auth.uid`. Rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == investorId` for direct ownership. 2.  **Investment:** Stored under `/investorProfiles/{investorId}/investments/{investmentId}`. The `Investment` document already contains an `investorId` field. Rules can verify `request.auth.uid == investorId` (from path) and `resource.data.investorId == investorId` (from document field), ensuring ownership without needing to read the parent `InvestorProfile`. 3.  **InvestmentDailyMetric:** Stored under `/investorProfiles/{investorId}/investments/{investmentId}/investmentDailyMetrics/{metricId}`. To ensure authorization independence for this subcollection, the `InvestmentDailyMetric` document **MUST be denormalized to include the `investorId` field** (in addition to its existing `investmentId` field). This allows rules to verify ownership directly (`request.auth.uid == investorId` from path and `resource.data.investorId == investorId` from document field) without needing to fetch the parent `Investment` or `InvestorProfile` documents. 4.  **PortfolioDailyMetric:** Stored under `/investorProfiles/{investorId}/portfolioDailyMetrics/{metricId}`. The `PortfolioDailyMetric` document already contains an `investorId` field. Rules verify `request.auth.uid == investorId` (from path) and `resource.data.investorId == investorId` (from document field). QAPs (Rules are not Filters): The hierarchical path structure combined with denormalized authorization fields ensures secure and efficient `list` operations. For example, a client requesting `/investorProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/investments` is inherently querying only their own investments. Security rules then simply validate that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{investorId}` wildcard in the path, and that any denormalized `investorId` fields within the documents themselves also match. This means the database is only asked for data the user is authorized to see, eliminating the need for client-side filtering or costly rule-based filtering. The strict path-based ownership prevents unauthorized users from even attempting to query other users' data. The structural segregation means all documents within a given collection path share the same security posture, simplifying rule logic. For instance, all documents in `/investorProfiles/{investorId}/investments` are owned by `investorId`.
